summary: Each Spectra object contains an implementation of a MsBackend within its @backend slot which provides the MS data to the Spectra object.
The MsBackend virtual class defines the API that new backend classes need to implement in order to be used with the Spectra object.
Each backend must provide a minimum required set of core spectra variables listed by the coreSpectraVariables() function.
dataStorage and dataOrigin are special spectra variables that define where the data is stored and from where the data derived.
Both dataStorage and dataOrigin must be of type character and need to be defined by the backend.
MsBackend implementations can represent purely read-only data resources where only data accessor methods need to be implemented but not data replacement methods.
Whether a backend is read-only can be set with the @readonly slot of the virtual MsBackend class.
The default value for @readonly is FALSE, requiring all data replacement methods to be implemented.
For read-only backends (@readonly = TRUE) only the methods in the Required methods section need to be implemented.

The spectraData() method should return the full spectra data within a backend as a DataFrame object. The spectraData<- method should allow to replace the data within a backend by taking a DataFrame with the full data as input value. The spe
Each row in the DataFrame returned by spectraData() should represent one spectrum and each column a spectra variable.
Columns mz and intensity (if requested) must contain each a NumericList with the m/z and intensity values of the spectra.
The DataFrame returned by spectraData() must provide values for all requested spectra variables of the backend including the core spectra variables.

The spectraVariables() method should return a character vector with the names of all available spectra variables of the backend. The peaksVariables() is expected to return a character vector with the names of the peaks variables available i
Each MsBackend class must provide the core spectra variables in the correct data type.
Core spectra variables can be NA with the exception of the spectra variable dataStorage.
The backendInitialize() method is expected to be called after creating an instance of the backend class and should prepare the backend.
During backendInitialize() the special spectra variables dataStorage and dataOrigin are usually set.
peaksData() is expected to return a List of numerical matrices with columns being the requested peaks variables of one spectrum.
Backends must provide at least mz and intensity as peaks variables.
The extractByIndex() and [ methods allow to subset MsBackend objects.
Subsetting with extractByIndex() and [ must support duplication and extraction in any arbitrary order.
extractByIndex() only supports to subset with an integer index while [ should support to subset by indices or logical vectors.
An error should be thrown if indices are out of bounds but the method should support returning an empty backend with [integer()].
The backendMerge() method merges MsBackend objects of the same type into a single instance.
The spectraNames() method can be used to extract optional names or IDs for individual spectra of a backend, or NULL if not set.
With the $<- method, the length() of value must match the number of spectra represented by the backend.
The number of spectra before and after calling spectraData<- method on an object has to be the same.
The intensity<- method should allow to replace only the values of the intensities but must not change the number of intensities per spectrum.
The peaksData<- method should support changing the number of peaks per spectrum such as due to filtering.
The selectSpectraVariables() function should allow to reduce the information within the backend to the selected spectra variables.
The selectSpectraVariables() is equivalent to a subset by columns or variables.
The $ method is expected to extract a single spectra variable from a backend.
Each MsBackend must support extracting the core spectra variables with the $ method even if no data might be available for that variable.
The $ method should check if the requested spectra variable is available and should throw an error otherwise.
The default implementation for peaksVariables() returns c("mz", "intensity") for MsBackend.
